commit_scope: Release GET /api/agent/tasks/{task_id} from Python fanout into the existing BML native task-detail handler while keeping reserved task collection subroutes outside the detail match.

commands: curl -sS --max-time 5 https://pulse.coherencycoin.com/pulse/now | jq '{overall, silences: (.ongoing_silences | length), silent_organs: [.organs[] | select(.status != "breathing") | .name], strained: [.organs[] | select(.status == "strained") | {name,detail,latency_ms,last_sample_at}]}'

form-cli ask "For this Coherence Network task, user asks to tend Python code and convert it to Form native. Wellness shows endpoint_ideas fallback strain, 799 Python API routes, and missing primitive registry entries form_compile form_walk str_byte_at. What is the smallest bounded Form-native migration to do now, with files and proof command?"

python3 scripts/runtime_surface_report.py --json | python3 -c 'import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); paths=set(r.split(" ",1)[1] if " " in r else r for r in d.get("kernel_first_capable_route_names", [])); print({"capable_routes": d.get("kernel_first_capable_routes"), "has_agent_task_detail": "/api/agent/tasks/{task_id}" in paths, "has_agent_task_task_star": "/api/agent/tasks/task_*" in paths})'

cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/json.fk form-stdlib/cache.fk form-stdlib/form-ontology-loader.fk form-stdlib/source-compiler.fk form-stdlib/kernel-http.fk form-stdlib/language-model.fk form-stdlib/tests/source-language-route-class-template-band.fk
local_validation.notes: Prompt guide passed on the fresh worktree. Wellness reported the known bootstrap/native migration pressure and a live witness strain on endpoint_ideas fallback. form-cli returned no local answer, so route selection used native-route goal evidence. Before the change, GET /api/agent/tasks/{task_id} was the highest observed Python fanout route in the web_api window. After the change, native_route_goal_loop classified GET /api/agent/tasks/{task_id} as kernel-native-high-grammar, and observed high-grammar share rose to 95.60%. Runtime-surface report showed capable_routes=104, has_agent_task_detail=true, and has_agent_task_task_star=false. The route-goal classifier and Hostinger ingress proof now reserve /api/agent/tasks/attention and /api/agent/tasks/count for their existing Python collection handlers until those get dedicated native BML handlers. The Form source route-template band returned 8389697 with 1 ok and 0 divergent. The worktree lacks a prebuilt form-kernel-rust release binary, so the direct Rust route-file check was not used as pass evidence.

ci_validation.notes: Not pushed when this evidence was written; CI had not run yet.

deploy_validation.summary: No deploy had run when this evidence was written.

phase_gate.gate: Observed agent task detail reads should enter the BML native front door for generic task id shapes, not only ids beginning task_, while reserved task collection subroutes stay on their current handlers.
phase_gate.next: Push, open PR, watch CI, merge, deploy, and verify the public task-detail route returns X-Form-Router: native-kernel for a live non-task_ id; verify reserved /attention and /count were not captured by the task-detail handler.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: GET /api/agent/tasks/{task_id} is now represented as a generic BML RouteCell and the Hostinger Traefik batch routes non-reserved single-segment task ids to the BML front door. The existing api_agent_task handler therefore covers observed non-task_ task ids instead of sending them to Python fanout, while /api/agent/tasks/attention and /api/agent/tasks/count remain outside the detail route.
test_flows: Route goal classification sees /api/agent/tasks/agent-anything as kernel-native-high-grammar.
Runtime-surface capable routes include /api/agent/tasks/{task_id} and no longer include /api/agent/tasks/task_*.
Hostinger deploy path coverage accepts the generic task-detail PathRegexp.
e2e_validation.summary: The local route-release proof passed and removed the observed Python fanout classification for agent task detail reads.
